commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The next action is contributor proof or maintainer review; there is no narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to produce on this PR.; Cleared: The diff only changes clipboard service logic and focused tests; it adds no dependency, credential, network, CI, release, or package execution surface. commands.review_summary:
The PR updates `ClipboardService.set(_:)` to count companion `alsoText` bytes in the size guard, reuse plain-text UTI detection, and add two focused clipboard regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main excludes `alsoText` from the size total and only uses `UTType.plainText.identifier` for the set-result preview branch. I did not run the Swift tests because this review is constrained to read-only inspection.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The next action is contributor proof or maintainer review; there is no narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to produce on this PR.

target.title: fix(clipboard): validate alternate text payloads
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #176.

`ClipboardService.set(_:)` now:

- includes `alsoText` bytes in the large-payload guard.
- treats `public.utf8-plain-text` as a plain-text representation for `.string` fallback and result preview.

## Verification

Run in `/Users/ninja/Desktop/peekaboo-pr-clipboard` on June 5, 2026.

```console
$ swift test --package-path Core/PeekabooAutomationKit --no-parallel --filter ClipboardWriteRequestTests
Test Suite 'ClipboardWriteRequestTests' passed
Executed 3 tests, with 0 failures (0 unexpected)

$ swiftlint lint --config .swiftlint.yml Core/PeekabooAutomationKit/Sources/PeekabooAutomationKit/Services/System/ClipboardService.swift Core/PeekabooAutomationKit/Tests/PeekabooAutomationKitTests/ClipboardWriteRequestTests.swift
Done linting! Found 0 violations, 0 serious in 2 files.

$ swiftformat Core/PeekabooAutomationKit/Sources/PeekabooAutomationKit/Services/System/ClipboardService.swift Core/PeekabooAutomationKit/Tests/PeekabooAutomationKitTests/ClipboardWriteRequestTests.swift
SwiftFormat completed in 0.04s.
0/2 files formatted.
```
